lessons.title: Had = Have/Has का Past Tense
lessons.theory: 'Had' is the past tense of both 'have' and 'has'. Just like 'have/has' is used for present possession, 'had' is used for past possession. The key rule: for ALL subjects (I, he, she, it, we, they, you), we use 'had' in past tense — there is no 'haded' or 'had have'. It is always just 'had'.
rules: I have → I had
He/She/It has → He/She/It had
We/They/You have → We/They/You had
Same form for all subjects — no exceptions!

examples.hindi: मेरे पास एक कार है।
मेरे पास एक कार थी।
उसके पास पैसे हैं।
उसके पास पैसे थे।
उनके पास एक घर है।
उनके पास एक घर था।
कंपनी के पास 100 कर्मचारी हैं।
कंपनी के पास 100 कर्मचारी थे।

lessons.keyPoint: Had never changes. I had, he had, she had, they had — always the same!

lessons.title: Past Possession — मेरे पास था/थी
lessons.theory: We use 'had' to describe things we owned, possessed, or experienced in the past. This covers physical objects (car, house, phone), relationships (a friend, a boss), qualities (a problem, an idea), and experiences (a meeting, a dream). In Hindi: 'मेरे पास ... था/थी/थे' = 'I had ...'
rules: Subject + had + noun/object
Use for physical possession: I had a bike, She had a laptop
Use for relationships: He had a girlfriend, We had a teacher
Use for abstract things: They had a plan, I had an idea

examples.hindi: मेरे पास एक मोटरसाइकिल थी।
उसके पास एक लैपटॉप था।
हमारे पास एक बड़ा घर था।
उनके पास बहुत पैसे थे।
examples.english: They had a lot of money.

examples.hindi: उसके पास एक अच्छा बॉस था।
मेरे पास एक पुरानी किताब थी।
कंपनी के पास एक बड़ी टीम थी।
examples.english: The company had a large team.

examples.hindi: बच्चे के पास बहुत खिलौने थे।
उसके पास एक अच्छा विचार था।
मेरे पास कोई समस्या नहीं थी।

lessons.keyPoint: Had = था/थी/थे (past possession). Use it whenever something existed in the past.

lessons.title: Past Perfect — Had + V3
lessons.theory: Past Perfect is used when one action happened BEFORE another past action. Structure: Subject + had + V3 (third form of verb). In Hindi, this often translates as 'मैंने ... कर लिया था' or 'वह ... जा चुका था'. This tense shows that something was already completed before something else happened in the past.
rules: Subject + had + V3 (past participle)
Used to show 'earlier past' — something that happened before another past event
Common V3 forms: eaten, gone, finished, done, written, seen, spoken
Often used with 'already', 'just', 'never', 'before', 'by the time'

examples.hindi: मैंने खाना खा लिया था।
वह जा चुका था।
हमने काम खत्म कर लिया था।
उसने रिपोर्ट लिख ली थी।
बारिश रुक गई थी।
वे पहले ही सो गए थे।
मीटिंग खत्म हो गई थी।
ट्रेन निकल चुकी थी।
उसने गलती स्वीकार कर ली थी।
टीम ने प्रोजेक्ट पूरा कर लिया था।
examples.english: The team had completed the project.

examples.hindi: मैंने यह फिल्म पहले देख ली थी।
examples.english: I had watched this film before.

examples.hindi: वह कभी विदेश नहीं गई थी।

lessons.keyPoint: Had + V3 = something that was already done before another past event.

lessons.title: Negative — Didn't Have / Hadn't
lessons.theory: To make 'had' negative in past possession, use 'didn't have' (did not have). To make Past Perfect negative, use 'hadn't' (had not + V3). Important: Never say 'didn't had' — that is wrong! Always use 'didn't have' for possession and 'hadn't + V3' for past perfect.
rules: Past Possession Negative: Subject + didn't have + object
Past Perfect Negative: Subject + hadn't + V3
WRONG: 'He didn't had a car' | CORRECT: 'He didn't have a car'
WRONG: 'She hadn't ate' | CORRECT: 'She hadn't eaten'

examples.hindi: मेरे पास पैसे नहीं थे।
उसके पास फोन नहीं था।
हमारे पास समय नहीं था।
उन्होंने खाना नहीं खाया था।
वह अभी तक नहीं आई थी।
मैंने उसे पहले नहीं देखा था।
टीम ने रिपोर्ट नहीं बनाई थी।
examples.english: The team hadn't made the report.

examples.hindi: मेरे पास कोई अनुभव नहीं था।
उसने माफी नहीं मांगी थी।
कंपनी के पास बजट नहीं था।
examples.english: The company didn't have a budget.

lessons.keyPoint: Possession negative = didn't have. Perfect negative = hadn't + V3. Never 'didn't had'!

lessons.title: Questions — Did you have? / Had you...?
lessons.theory: To ask questions about past possession, use 'Did + subject + have + ...?' For Past Perfect questions, use 'Had + subject + V3 + ...?' Note that in questions, the main verb goes back to base form after 'did' — so it is 'did you have' not 'did you had'.
rules: Possession Question: Did + subject + have + object?
Perfect Question: Had + subject + V3?
WRONG: 'Did you had a car?' | CORRECT: 'Did you have a car?'
Short answers: Yes, I did / No, I didn't | Yes, I had / No, I hadn't

examples.hindi: क्या तुम्हारे पास एक कार थी?
क्या उसके पास पैसे थे?
क्या तुमने खाना खा लिया था?
क्या वह जा चुका था?
क्या उन्होंने बैठक में भाग लिया था?
क्या तुम्हारे पास कोई सुझाव था?
क्या रिपोर्ट तैयार हो गई थी?
क्या तुमने पहले यह काम किया था?
examples.english: Had you done this work before?

examples.hindi: क्या उनके पास कोई योजना थी?
क्या टीम ने प्रोजेक्ट पूरा किया था?
examples.english: Had the team completed the project?

lessons.keyPoint: Did + have (not 'did + had') for possession questions. Had + V3 for perfect questions.

lessons.title: Sequence of Events — When I reached, he had already left
lessons.theory: One of the most natural uses of Past Perfect is showing the order of two past events. The earlier event uses Past Perfect (had + V3), and the later event uses Simple Past. Common connectors: when, by the time, before, after, already, just. This helps listeners understand WHICH thing happened first.
rules: Earlier event: Past Perfect (had + V3)
Later event: Simple Past (V2)
Connectors: when, by the time, before, after
Key words: already, just, never, yet

examples.hindi: जब मैं पहुँचा, वह पहले ही जा चुका था।
examples.english: When I reached, he had already left.

examples.hindi: जब उसने फोन किया, मैं सो चुका था।
examples.english: When she called, I had already slept.

examples.hindi: जब तक हम स्टेशन पहुँचे, ट्रेन जा चुकी थी।
examples.english: By the time we reached the station, the train had left.

examples.hindi: इससे पहले कि मैं कुछ कहता, उसने माफी मांग ली थी।
examples.english: Before I could say anything, he had apologized.

examples.hindi: जब मैंने रिपोर्ट जमा की, वह पहले ही देख चुका था।
examples.english: When I submitted the report, he had already reviewed it.

examples.hindi: जब बॉस आया, सब काम हो चुका था।
examples.english: When the boss came, all the work had been done.

examples.hindi: डॉक्टर के पहुँचने से पहले, मरीज़ ठीक हो गया था।
examples.english: Before the doctor arrived, the patient had recovered.

examples.hindi: जब तक मुझे पता चला, मौका जा चुका था।
examples.english: By the time I found out, the opportunity had gone.

examples.hindi: जब हमने मीटिंग शुरू की, मैंने पहले ही नोट्स तैयार कर लिए थे।
examples.english: When we started the meeting, I had already prepared the notes.

examples.hindi: उसने नौकरी छोड़ने से पहले, उसने एक नई नौकरी ढूंढ ली थी।
examples.english: Before she left the job, she had found a new one.

lessons.keyPoint: Earlier action = had + V3. Later action = Simple Past. Use 'when', 'by the time', 'before' to connect.

lessons.title: Conditional — If I had money, I would buy it
lessons.theory: The Third Conditional uses 'had' to talk about imaginary past situations. Structure: If + subject + had + V3, subject + would have + V3. This is used to express regret, hypothetical situations, and 'what if' scenarios. A simpler form also exists: If + subject + had + noun, subject + would + verb (Second Conditional style with past possession).
rules: Third Conditional: If + had + V3, would have + V3
If I had known, I would have told you.
Simpler form: If I had money, I would buy it.
Shows unreal/hypothetical past or past conditions

examples.hindi: अगर मेरे पास पैसे होते, मैं यह खरीदता।
examples.english: If I had money, I would buy it.

examples.hindi: अगर मुझे पता होता, मैं तुम्हें बताता।
examples.english: If I had known, I would have told you.

examples.hindi: अगर उसके पास समय होता, वह आता।
examples.english: If he had time, he would come.

examples.hindi: अगर मैंने मेहनत की होती, मैं पास हो जाता।
examples.english: If I had worked hard, I would have passed.

examples.hindi: अगर उसने सुना होता, गलती नहीं होती।
examples.english: If she had listened, the mistake wouldn't have happened.

examples.hindi: अगर हमारे पास एक प्लान होता, हम सफल होते।
examples.english: If we had a plan, we would have succeeded.

examples.hindi: अगर मैंने पहले आवेदन किया होता, नौकरी मिल जाती।
examples.english: If I had applied earlier, I would have got the job.

examples.hindi: अगर उसने ईमेल पढ़ा होता, वह जानता।
examples.english: If he had read the email, he would have known.

lessons.keyPoint: If + had (+ V3 or noun) → would + (have + V3 or verb). Used for unreal/hypothetical situations.

lessons.title: Common Phrases with Had
lessons.theory: Many everyday English expressions use 'had'. These are idiomatic and natural phrases that native speakers use constantly. Memorizing these will make your English sound much more natural in both casual and professional settings.

phrases.hindi: नाश्ता/दोपहर का खाना/रात का खाना खाया

phrases.example: We had a team meeting yesterday.
He had a problem with the system.
We had a good time at the party.
I had a strange dream last night.
She had a great idea for the project.
He had a cold last week.
She had no choice but to resign.
I had a look at the report.
I had enough of his excuses.

lessons.keyPoint: Learn these common 'had' phrases to sound natural in everyday and professional English.

lessons.title: Office and Professional Use of Had
lessons.theory: In office settings, 'had' is extremely common. It is used in emails, reports, meetings, and professional conversation to describe past situations, completed tasks, and past challenges. This lesson focuses on professional English using 'had'.

examples.hindi: हमारी कल एक टीम मीटिंग थी।
examples.english: We had a team meeting yesterday.

examples.hindi: प्रोजेक्ट में कुछ तकनीकी समस्याएं थीं।
examples.english: The project had some technical issues.

examples.hindi: पिछले साल कंपनी के पास 500 क्लाइंट थे।
examples.english: Last year, the company had 500 clients.

examples.hindi: जब तक मैं पहुँचा, निर्णय हो चुका था।
examples.english: By the time I arrived, the decision had been made.

examples.hindi: उसने लक्ष्य पहले ही पूरे कर लिए थे।
examples.english: She had already achieved the targets.

examples.hindi: ग्राहक को पहले से एक खराब अनुभव था।
examples.english: The client had a bad experience earlier.

examples.hindi: कर्मचारी के पास 5 साल का अनुभव था।
examples.english: The employee had 5 years of experience.

examples.hindi: जब बॉस ने जाँच की, टीम ने काम खत्म कर लिया था।
examples.english: When the boss checked, the team had finished the work.

examples.hindi: हमने पहले भी ऐसी समस्या देखी थी।
examples.english: We had seen such a problem before.

examples.hindi: पिछले साल हमारे पास ज़्यादा बजट था।
examples.english: Last year, we had a bigger budget.

professionalTips: Use 'had' in past reports to describe what existed at a specific time
Use 'had already + V3' to show proactive completion of tasks
Use 'hadn't' to professionally explain why something wasn't done
'Had a meeting', 'had a call', 'had a deadline' are very common in office English
lessons.keyPoint: In office English, 'had' is used constantly for past states, past tasks, and timeline reporting.
